title: 21:31 â August 27, 2021
body_text:
21:31 â August 27, 2021
e-Signature Font Issue Resolved
In response to an ongoing technical issue for OneSpan and DocuSign e-signature providers, we have changed all our fillable PDF form field fonts from Courier to Arial.
Important
: Please note that the date on the Forms page of secure site has been updated, as it is an automatic feature. We have not changed the revision dates on any forms themselves.
About the Issue
Starting at the end of July, several credit unions reported an issue where, when sending documents for e-signature using one of the above providers and either Chrome or Edge, all information in fillable PDF fields disappeared. This happened because our standard field font, Courier, does not embed in the documents. To learn more about embedding fonts in PDFs, you can read
https://helpx.adobe.com/acrobat/using/pdf-fonts.html
. OneSpan and DocuSign are both working to allow non-embedded fonts to show in these browsers.
How to Change Fonts Yourself
You can check to see which fonts are embedded in your documents by opening your PDF and going to
File > Properties > Fonts
:
If you have custom forms or documents with fillable fields that have non-embedded fonts, you can use the
Adobe Acrobat Action Wizard
to change them in all your documents at once. PCD has created an Action that you can import directly into the Action Wizard, available
here
. You can also create your own with
these instructions
.
We recommend making a backup of all documents before making any changes.
Changes to the Document Library
All fillable PDF forms have had their text field font changed from Courier to Arial.
If you have any questions regarding this release, please feel free to contact us:
Product Compliance & Design
